key_judgments.judgment: Benchmark-reality gaps expose unmonitored deployment risks

weekly_brief_draft: No dominant headline signal emerges this week from provided documents, with activity limited to model updates and sector deployments lacking governance substance. Google Gemini introduces interactive 3D models for exploratory interfaces, Meta launches Muse Spark across its ecosystem for multimodal agentic coordination, and Z.ai open-sources GLM-5.1 for 8-hour autonomous engineering under MIT license, signaling T2/T3 acceleration without safety disclosures. Microsoft Copilot Health fuses 50k+ provider records with wearables, while Salesforce Slackbot gains 30+ capabilities as its fastest-adopted feature, evidencing enterprise productivity surges but highlighting capability-deployment gaps where real-world accuracy trails benchmarks by 20-40%. EU AI Act Standards Vacuum remains active ahead of August high-risk obligations, with no Official Journal harmonised standards despite CEN-CENELEC drafts. Risk vectors hold steady: Safety Gap elevated from agentic failures, Standards Vacuum high creating GPAI arbitrage, Benchmark Saturation moderate as MMLU plateaus drive evals like Humanity's Last Exam. Enterprise maturity lags at DAIMI 2.93 per Digitopia, inference costs rise yet stay labor-cheap per Redwood, amplifying energy nexus pressures. OpenAI's vulnerability model flags misuse potential in offensive capabilities. Concentration stable around Anthropic's METR-leading Opus 4.6, regulatory trackers on pace with EU GPAI Code advancing to Q2 finalisation. Governance health composite at 0.35 reflects standards drag. Watch CEN-CENELEC progress, GPAI systemic-risk designations, and METR extensions beyond 12-hour horizons for capability-governance velocity mismatches.

cross_monitor_candidates.signal: Accuracy gaps and inference costs raise inefficient energy footprint
